issues.title: bibs: update_marc_field silently deletes repeated instances of a repeatable MARC field (data loss)

tests.scope: Live-SANDBOX proof of the #184 fix: updating one instance of a repeatable tag must not delete the others. Creates a throwaway seed bib carrying three 650 subject headings (Physics / Astrophysics / Cosmology), calls update_marc_field(created_mms_id, '650', {'a': 'Physics -- data reduction'}) with the default non-destructive mode (replace_first), re-fetches the record and asserts all three 650s survive with only the first occurrence rewritten. Pre-fix, _build_updated_marc_xml replaced the first 650 and continue-d past the rest, collapsing three subject headings to one silently. The starting state is fully self-contained (a bib this test creates), so no operator record is touched; cleanup deletes the seed bib. The exact XML-rebuild logic is pinned offline by tests/unit/regressions/test_issue_184.py; this is end-to-end corroboration through a real Alma GET+PUT round-trip.
pythonCalls: seed_xml = '<bib><record><leader>00000nam a2200000 a 4500</leader><controlfield tag="008">230101s2023    xx            000 0 eng d</controlfield><datafield tag="245" ind1="1" ind2="0"><subfield code="a">AlmaAPITK MARC-fix seed 184</subfield></datafield><datafield tag="650" ind1=" " ind2="0"><subfield code="a">Physics</subfield></datafield><datafield tag="650" ind1=" " ind2="0"><subfield code="a">Astrophysics</subfield></datafield><datafield tag="650" ind1=" " ind2="0"><subfield code="a">Cosmology</subfield></datafield></record></bib>'
record = root.find('record') if root.tag == 'bib' else root
subj_650 = [sf.text for df in record.findall("datafield[@tag='650']") for sf in df.findall("subfield[@code='a']")]
passCriteria: create_response.success is True and created_mms_id is a non-empty string — the throwaway seed bib with three 650 subject headings was created in SANDBOX.
update_response.success is True — the default replace_first update of the 650 tag completed.
len(subj_650) == 3 — all three 650 subject headings survive the update (pre-fix this collapsed to a single 650: the #184 silent-data-loss symptom).
subj_650[0] == 'Physics -- data reduction' — the first occurrence was rewritten in place.
'Astrophysics' in subj_650 and 'Cosmology' in subj_650 — the two untargeted subject headings are preserved verbatim.
No AlmaAPIError, AlmaValidationError, or AlmaRateLimitError is raised during create, update, or retrieve.

tests.scope: Live-SANDBOX proof of the #184 'supported non-destructive append' AC. Creates a throwaway seed bib with three 650s, calls update_marc_field(created_mms_id, '650', {'a': 'Particle physics'}, mode='append'), re-fetches and asserts the record now carries four 650s — the original three intact plus the appended one. Exercises the explicit mode='append' path that lets a caller add an occurrence of a repeatable field without deleting existing ones. Self-contained starting state; cleanup deletes the seed bib.
pythonCalls: seed_xml = '<bib><record><leader>00000nam a2200000 a 4500</leader><controlfield tag="008">230101s2023    xx            000 0 eng d</controlfield><datafield tag="245" ind1="1" ind2="0"><subfield code="a">AlmaAPITK MARC-fix seed 184</subfield></datafield><datafield tag="650" ind1=" " ind2="0"><subfield code="a">Physics</subfield></datafield><datafield tag="650" ind1=" " ind2="0"><subfield code="a">Astrophysics</subfield></datafield><datafield tag="650" ind1=" " ind2="0"><subfield code="a">Cosmology</subfield></datafield></record></bib>'
record = root.find('record') if root.tag == 'bib' else root
subj_650 = [sf.text for df in record.findall("datafield[@tag='650']") for sf in df.findall("subfield[@code='a']")]
passCriteria: create_response.success is True and created_mms_id is a non-empty string — the seed bib with three 650s was created.
update_response.success is True — the mode='append' update completed.
len(subj_650) == 4 — append kept all three original 650s and added exactly one more.
'Physics' in subj_650 and 'Astrophysics' in subj_650 and 'Cosmology' in subj_650 and 'Particle physics' in subj_650 — every original heading survives and the new one is present.
No AlmaAPIError, AlmaValidationError, or AlmaRateLimitError is raised during create, update, or retrieve.

unmappable.ac: R10 regression test under `tests/unit/regressions/` reproduces the 3→1 collapse; fails on current `main`, passes after the fix.
unmappable.reason: This AC is a local pytest-suite deliverable, not an observable SANDBOX API behaviour. The regression file tests/unit/regressions/test_issue_184.py is present in the chunk diff and is verified by running `pytest tests/unit/regressions/test_issue_184.py` (it fails on current `main` and passes on this branch) — nothing about the presence/pass-state of a unit test is visible via any Alma REST call. The live behavioural facet this regression pins IS separately exercised against SANDBOX by t-184-1 (three 650s survive a single-instance update) and t-184-2 (append adds without deleting).

issues.title: bibs: MARC editing path cannot represent repeated subfield codes (dict-typed subfields)

tests.scope: Live-SANDBOX proof of the #185 fix: the editing path must accept an ordered list of [code, value] pairs (not a dict) and preserve repeated subfield codes. Creates a throwaway seed bib with a single 650, then calls update_marc_field(created_mms_id, '650', [['a','Science'],['x','History'],['x','20th century']]) — a shape a dict could never express (two '$x'). Re-fetches and asserts the 650 comes back as $a Science / $x History / $x 20th century, in order, with BOTH $x values present. Pre-fix the dict-typed path silently dropped the second $x at dict-construction time. Self-contained starting state; cleanup deletes the seed bib. The exact ordering/normalisation is pinned offline by tests/unit/regressions/test_issue_185.py.
pythonCalls: seed_xml = '<bib><record><leader>00000nam a2200000 a 4500</leader><controlfield tag="008">230101s2023    xx            000 0 eng d</controlfield><datafield tag="245" ind1="1" ind2="0"><subfield code="a">AlmaAPITK MARC-fix seed 185</subfield></datafield><datafield tag="650" ind1=" " ind2="0"><subfield code="a">Science</subfield></datafield></record></bib>'
record = root.find('record') if root.tag == 'bib' else root
pairs_650 = [(sf.get('code'), sf.text) for df in record.findall("datafield[@tag='650']") for sf in df.findall('subfield')]
passCriteria: create_response.success is True and created_mms_id is a non-empty string — the seed bib was created.
update_response.success is True — update_marc_field accepted an ordered list of [code, value] pairs (not a dict) and Alma accepted the resulting field.
pairs_650 == [('a', 'Science'), ('x', 'History'), ('x', '20th century')] — the two repeated $x subfields both round-trip, in order.
[v for c, v in pairs_650 if c == 'x'] == ['History', '20th century'] — both distinct $x values survive (impossible via the pre-fix dict-typed path).
No AlmaAPIError, AlmaValidationError, or AlmaRateLimitError is raised during create, update, or retrieve.

The live behavioural facet this regression pins IS separately exercised against SANDBOX by t-185-1 (two $x subfields round-trip in order through a real Alma GET+PUT).

issues.title: bibs: double-escaping of ampersand/lt/gt corrupts field content in MARC editing path

tests.scope: Live-SANDBOX proof of the #186 fix: a field value containing '&', '<' and '>' must round-trip through update_marc_field unmangled (escaped exactly once). Creates a throwaway seed bib with a 245, calls update_marc_field(created_mms_id, '245', [['a','Law & order <suppl> report']], ind1='1', ind2='0'), re-fetches and asserts the decoded 245 $a is the exact literal and the raw re-fetched MARCXML shows no doubly-escaped '&amp;'. Pre-fix, _sanitize_xml_text pre-escaped '&'->'&' and then ElementTree escaped again, so Alma stored 'Law & order' — a mangled ampersand. Self-contained starting state; cleanup deletes the seed bib. Single-level-escaping is also pinned offline by tests/unit/regressions/test_issue_186.py.
pythonCalls: seed_xml = '<bib><record><leader>00000nam a2200000 a 4500</leader><controlfield tag="008">230101s2023    xx            000 0 eng d</controlfield><datafield tag="245" ind1="1" ind2="0"><subfield code="a">AlmaAPITK MARC-fix seed 186</subfield></datafield></record></bib>'
update_response = bibs.update_marc_field(created_mms_id, '245', [['a', 'Law & order <suppl> report']], ind1='1', ind2='0')
record = root.find('record') if root.tag == 'bib' else root
title_245 = [sf.text for df in record.findall("datafield[@tag='245']") for sf in df.findall("subfield[@code='a']")]
passCriteria: create_response.success is True and created_mms_id is a non-empty string — the seed bib was created.
update_response.success is True — the 245 update completed.

title_245 == ['Law & order <suppl> report'] — the value round-trips with '&', '<' and '>' intact (decoded exactly once). Pre-fix double-escaping would have stored a literal '&' and the decoded re-fetch would read 'Law & order ...'.
'&amp;' not in raw_anies — the raw re-fetched MARCXML carries a single level of escaping, never a doubly-escaped entity.
No AlmaAPIError, AlmaValidationError, or AlmaRateLimitError is raised during create, update, or retrieve.
